title: cerastium viscosum var. alsinoides
intro: Cerastium viscosum var. alsinoides, commonly known as sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) native to Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium viscosum var. alsinoides include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, salve, or infused oil for topical use, or as a tea for internal consumption.
